@graph.name: Mercedes SL320 CDI Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes SL320 CDI engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned SL320 CDI (OM648) engine typically costs £4,500–£8,500 supply only. A replacement engine for a diesel luxury car can cost $7,000–$15,000+. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes SL320 CDI engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM648 is a robust cast‑iron straight‑six diesel with a ~400,000 km lifespan. It is known for its smooth power delivery, linear torque, and durability. However, it has well‑documented issues: common‑rail fuel system failures, injector problems, swirl flap motor and linkage failures, turbo VNT sticking, and DPF blockage. The R230 platform's ABC suspension and SBC brakes are the real concerns.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes SL320 CDI?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common‑rail fuel system failures (fuel rail pressure sensor, fuel shut‑off valve), injector issues (rough idle, cold‑start misfires), swirl flap motor and linkage failure (limp mode), turbocharger VNT sticking (whistling, power loss), DPF blockage, and R230‑specific ABC suspension and SBC brake issues. See the Era Map above for detailed advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes SL320 CDI engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well‑kept SL320 CDI (values £8,000–£20,000+ depending on condition), a £5,000–£6,000 reconditioned engine is 25–60% of the vehicle's value — often worth it for a solid example. The Mercedes scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing. Given the SL320 CDI's rarity and status as a future classic, engine replacement is often worth it for a good example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes SL320 CDI engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code OM648 is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The SL320 CDI was produced from 2005 to 2006 with the 3.2‑litre straight‑six diesel engine producing 224 bhp. The OM648 is also found in the S‑Class (S320 CDI), E‑Class (E320 CDI), and CLS‑Class (CLS320 CDI). Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM648 injectors, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injector issues are always repairable without replacing the engine — if caught early. Injector replacement costs £400–£1,200. Injectors can last 300,000–400,000 km with good maintenance. However, if a faulty injector causes engine damage, more extensive work may be required. At the first sign of rough idle or misfires, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes SL320 CDI Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Budget priority; finding a good donor OM648 is becoming harder
OM648 engines are no longer produced new; reconditioned units are the standard

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the SL320 CDI OM648 era (2005–2006). A replacement engine for a diesel luxury car can cost $7,000–$15,000+. Mercedes SL320 CDI engine replacements are rare given the limited production of this variant.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SL320 CDI engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £85–£130/hr (independent) or £140–£200/hr (London/SE dealer). The R230's complex engine bay and the OM648's inline‑six layout add significant labour. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare SL320 CDI engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Common‑Rail Fuel System Failures (OM648)
problems.symptoms: Hard starting, rough idle, loss of power, engine warning light, fuel rail pressure sensor faults, fuel shut‑off valve failure.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,500 (fuel rail pressure sensor, fuel shut‑off valve, or fuel pump repair)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,500 (if fuel system failure leads to engine damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if hard starting or power loss occurs

problems.recommendation: The OM648's common‑rail fuel system is the most frequent source of problems. Fuel rail pressure sensors and fuel shut‑off valves are known to fail. The high‑pressure fuel pump is driven off the timing chain and can cause issues. If you experience hard starting, loss of power, or rough idle, have the fuel system inspected immediately. Regular fuel filter changes and using high‑quality diesel are the best prevention.

problems.title: Fuel Injector Issues (OM648)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfires on cold start, uneven idle that smooths out after warm‑up, engine vibration at idle, poor fuel economy.
problems.typicalMileage: 100,000–200,000 miles — injectors can last 300,000–400,000 km if well‑maintained
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,500 (if injector failure leads to engine damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can affect drivability and fuel economy

problems.recommendation: The OM648's injectors can develop issues, with symptoms including rough idle and cold‑start misfires. Injector correction values can be too high, indicating wear. A leaking injector can cause nailing sounds from overpressure or weak injectors. If you experience rough idle or vibration, have the injectors checked. Using high‑quality diesel and regular fuel filter changes helps extend injector life.

problems.title: Swirl Flap Motor & Linkage Failure (OM648)
problems.symptoms: Limp mode, loss of power, check engine light, P2015 fault code (swirl flap position), poor fuel economy.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (swirl flap motor replacement or resistor bypass)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,500 (if swirl flap failure leads to engine damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if limp mode occurs

problems.recommendation: The OM648's swirl flap system is a known weak point. Swirl flap motors fail, causing limp mode. The linkage arms can break or stick. A common fix is to remove the swirl flaps and use a resistor to bypass the motor. Some owners delete the flaps entirely by swapping to a non‑swirl‑flap intake manifold. If you experience limp mode, have the swirl flap system inspected and consider a delete if appropriate.

problems.title: Turbocharger VNT Sticking (OM648)
problems.symptoms: Whistling or whining noise from the engine bay, loss of power, reduced boost, smoke under acceleration, laggy response.
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,500 (if turbo failure sends debris into the engine)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if whistling or power loss occurs

problems.recommendation: The OM648's variable geometry turbocharger (VGT) can suffer from sticking vanes due to carbon buildup. This can cause whistling noises, loss of power, and reduced boost. VNT cleaning can restore function, but in severe cases, turbo replacement is required. Regular motorway driving and using premium diesel can help reduce carbon buildup.

problems.title: DPF Blockage (OM648 — Optional from 2004)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced power, increased fuel consumption, frequent regeneration cycles, limp mode.
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–150,000 miles (urban‑driven cars are at highest risk)
problems.repairCost: £200–£800 (DPF clean, forced regeneration, or sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if DPF warning appears

problems.recommendation: DPF clogging is common on diesel SLs, particularly on cars driven mostly in the city. A DPF was optional from 2004, so not all SL320 CDIs have one. If you see a DPF warning light, take the car for a sustained motorway run at 2,000–2,500 rpm. If the light persists, have the DPF inspected. Regular motorway driving and using premium diesel can help keep the DPF healthy.

problems.title: R230‑Specific ABC Suspension & SBC Brake Issues
problems.symptoms: Red warning triangle, suspension error messages, car sitting low, uneven ride height, brake warning lights, pumping brake pedal, "SBC Visit Workshop" message.
problems.repairCost: £500–£3,000+ (ABC pump, strut or SBC pump replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if red warning messages appear, stop driving and book an inspection immediately

problems.recommendation: The R230 SL is notorious for complex and expensive issues with the Active Body Control (ABC) suspension and Sensotronic Brake Control (SBC) system. SBC failures can lead to complete brake failure — a red warning triangle means stop driving. ABC struts and pumps fail regularly. If you're buying an SL320 CDI, ensure the ABC suspension and SBC system have been recently serviced or replaced. These systems are the Achilles' heel of the R230.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes SL320 CDI — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it before fuel system damage occurs
Delete or bypass is common and cost‑effective
Cleaning is cost‑effective; replacement if severe
Cleaning or forced regeneration is cost‑effective
Essential for safety; R230 Achilles' heel
OM648 failures are rare but catastrophic when they occur

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SL320 CDI? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes using Mercedes‑approved oil
* Swirl flap system deleted or functioning correctly
* ABC suspension recently serviced (struts, pump, fluid)
* SBC brake system recently serviced or replaced
* R230 platform: ensure ABC and SBC are in good working order
avoidIf: * No proof of regular maintenance — the OM648 is durable but unforgiving of neglect
* ABC suspension warning lights — very expensive repairs ahead
* SBC brake warning lights — safety‑critical failure imminent
* Swirl flap limp mode issues — work may be required
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — the OM648 can hide expensive faults
* "Cheap" examples — the purchase price is the least of your worries

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes SL320 CDI is a rare gem — a diesel‑powered R230 SL with a 3.2‑litre straight‑six engine that offers 224 bhp, 500 Nm of torque, and up to 35 mpg. It was produced for only two years (2005–2006), making it a rare sight on UK roads. The OM648 engine is a cast‑iron workhorse with a ~400,000 km lifespan — known for its smooth power delivery, linear torque, and durability. However, it has well‑documented issues: common‑rail fuel system failures, injector problems, swirl flap motor and linkage failures, turbo VNT sticking, and DPF blockage. The R230 platform itself is the real concern — the ABC suspension and SBC brake systems are expensive to repair and can fail catastrophically. As one owner put it: "The R230 is a very complex car". Buy on service history, prioritise a car with recent ABC and SBC work, and consider a swirl flap delete. This is a brilliant car — but it demands deep pockets and careful ownership.

cta.label: → Compare SL320 CDI engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes SL320 CDI (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any unusual noises (injector health indicator)
* Service History — Regular oil changes with Mercedes‑approved oil are absolutely essential
* Injector Health — Any rough idle or misfires? (injector failure warning)
* Swirl Flap System — Any limp mode or loss of power? (swirl flap motor warning)
* Turbo Health — Any whistling, power loss, or smoke? (turbo VNT sticking warning)
* DPF Health — Any warning lights? Ask about regeneration frequency; urban cars will have more issues
* ABC Suspension — Any red warning triangle? Car sitting low? (ABC failure warning)
* SBC Brakes — Any brake warnings or pumping pedal? (SBC failure — safety‑critical)

items.label: SECTION 9, META AND SCHEMA
items.value: Section 9 (Trust + Final CTA) and all the meta, Open Graph, and schema data.

items.label: The user's deep need here is efficiency and consistency. They're not just asking for more content; they're asking for the completion of a structured document according to a very specific, complex template. They likely manage multiple such pages and need a reliable, repeatable process. They might be an SEO specialist, a content manager, or an AI workflow operator responsible for generating large volumes of these pages. The identity could be someone in automotive marketing or a technical content generation role.
items.value: My response needs to pick up exactly where Part 1 left off. I need to ensure all the information from Part 1's Era Map, Step 0 checks, and specific engine details (OM648, R230 platform) is carried forward into Part 2. The user expects accuracy and adherence to the brand voice (confident, data-led, honest for Mercedes) and the document's strict formatting rules—like the 3 trust bullets, 50-60 character meta title, 145-160 character meta description, and the complete JSON-LD schema with all required blocks.

items.label: I should structure Part 2 in the exact sequence the prompt dictates: Section 9 content, Meta tags, Open Graph and Twitter Card data, the full JSON-LD schema array, and finally the Production Note with all the verification checks. The Production Note is crucial—it needs to confirm that the Service schema's price range spans the full history (based on Section 1B), that all engine codes in the schema match the Era Map, and that the reliability rating is appropriately marked. I also need to ensure the brand prefix (MB-) and specialist count (25+) are correctly applied for Mercedes.
items.value: The user's workflow seems to involve generating pages for a wide range of brands (Land Rover, Jaguar, BMW, Mercedes) and specific badges, as seen in the long history. They're systematically working through a Variant Page Inventory. My role is to act as a precise executor of the "AI Handover Document 2.0" rules, ensuring every generated page is complete, accurate, and ready for publication.
